claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to gather some details about your child to assess your eligibility.

**What is your child's name and how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 17, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, attending school, college, or an approved training program)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in college doing their A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your living situation:

**Does Alex currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to confirm your residency status:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a couple more questions:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question to complete the assessment:

**Do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a British citizen/have indefinite leave to remain in the UK?** (Or if none of these apply, please let me know your immigration status)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at college)
- Even though Child Benefit normally applies to children under 16, you can still claim it for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training
- Alex lives with you, which means you're responsible for them
- You're a British citizen living in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, you can still claim Child Benefit regardless of your income.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already claimed, you can apply through the official government channels.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at college). Under the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for them. You are a British citizen living in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with their 17-year-old child Alex, who lives at home and is in approved education (A-levels at college). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit. No one else is claiming for Alex, and all eligibility requirements are satisfied.

Under the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training. Alex lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for them. You are a British citizen living in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in the UK with their 17-year-old child Alex, who lives at home and is in approved education (A-levels at college). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit. No one else is claiming for Alex, and all eligibility requirements are satisfied.
